ChangeRequest.name: Distinction between 'negotiable' and 'non-negotiable' required for all types of `Securities`?
ChangeRequest.comment:
<!-- See: documentation on [Issues in GitLab](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/wikis/Issues-in-GitLab) -->
# Overview
My understanding is that the distinction between 'negotiable' and 'non-negotiable' is relevant only for `Debt securities` (not all types of `Securities`) in order to identify `Debt securities` which are classified as `BSI`.`Loans`.
If this understanding is correct the `Negotiable security position indicator` should be located / placed in the `Long debt security position` entity type, instead of its current location in the `Long security position` entity type - this change of course includes some renaming and moving the subtypes as well.
### Contact person
dominik.lin-ully@oenb.at
### Background information _[info]_
### Points/Questions to resolve
* check if the distinction between 'negotiable' and 'non-negotiable' `Security position`(s) is required for all types of `Securities` * if only required for `Debt securities`, please adjust the model accordingly
### Related issues
### Before closing checklist - [ ] Assignee for review - [ ] Assignee to close - [ ] Other actions
